context: slideTypeVocab.decorate() throws Error('Method not implemented.') — deciding whether to fix or leave it
choice: Leave it unfixed in this epic
rationale: vocab decomposes into MC child slides at runtime; in the Vue rendering path the Vocab instance's decorate() is never called — the MC components own all visual state. Fix would be a no-op.
alternatives: Fix by implementing decorate() as a no-op stub
Fix by delegating to MC child decorate()

context: mobile-drag-drop polyfill in package.json — deciding whether to remove it with the gap conversion
choice: Remove mobile-drag-drop when GapExercise.vue is wired (T-150)
rationale: SortableJS (via vue-draggable-plus) handles mobile touch natively including cross-list DnD. The polyfill was only needed because the old gap used HTML5 native drag-drop which has poor mobile support.
alternatives: Keep polyfill as belt-and-suspenders
Remove polyfill in cleanup phase (T-180)

context: imap SVG injection: keep @tanem/svg-injector or replace with native fetch + v-html
rationale: @tanem/svg-injector: zero new code, already a dependency, well-tested. Native fetch + v-html: removes a dependency, fewer moving parts, but requires ~10 lines of fetch/parse code. Either works correctly from onMounted.
alternatives: Always use svg-injector
Always use native fetch

context: TSK specified component paths under src/components/exercise/ and the helper under src/ts/utils/, but the repo has no top-level src/components/ — Vue components live under src/vue/components/
choice: Place exercise components under src/vue/components/exercise/; keep processOptions under the TS layer (src/ts/utils/processOptions.ts)
rationale: Matches the existing repo structure and CLAUDE.md's 'Vue presentation layer = src/vue/' rule; src/ts/utils/ is a clean framework-agnostic home that resolves cleanly given baseUrl=src/ts. Corrected all 8 path references in TSK-001.
alternatives: Follow TSK paths literally (create new top-level src/components/)

context: Earlier note claimed jQuery was needed by course-content load() so the npm package could not be dropped. Investigation found two independent jQuery presences: the npm package (only append/empty in the rendering path + one $.extend in saveData.ts) and a separate global lib/jquery.min.js in index.html used by course-content inline scripts
choice: Fully remove the npm jquery + @types/jquery in this epic (replace $.extend with [...(raw as SaveData[])]); defer global lib/jquery.min.js + course-content load() rewrite to PRD-002
rationale: The npm removal is low-risk (one shallow-array-copy swap covered by saveData.property.test.ts) and makes T-210 literally true. The global jQuery is course-content concern, spans ~10 course files, is regression-prone, and is outside PRD-001's exercise-rendering scope.
alternatives: Keep npm jquery, satisfy T-210 via 'unused by exercise path' escape hatch
Remove global jQuery + rewrite all course load() calls in this epic

context: Converting each exercise type to a Vue component removes the legacy DOM (#btn0, #continueBtn, #ans0, #w4, startOver) that cypress/e2e/example.cy.ts asserts against, yet yarn test:all (which includes e2e) is the per-phase gate
choice: New exercise components expose stable data-cy hooks; example.cy.ts selectors are updated in lockstep within each phase's wiring task
rationale: Keeps each phase gate green without preserving ugly legacy IDs on clean Vue components, and without deferring all e2e fixes to the end (which would leave the suite red across the whole conversion). See ADR-022.
alternatives: Preserve legacy element IDs on the new components so existing specs pass untouched
Rewrite the entire e2e suite once at the end

context: Containerized sessions: yarn test:all includes test:e2e but the Cypress binary was not installed and, once downloaded, extracted without an execute bit
choice: Document a Cypress prerequisite — yarn cypress install + chmod -R u+x on the cached binary — before the first gate
rationale: Verified empirically: after install + chmod, yarn cypress verify passes and the full e2e suite (6 specs) runs and passes headless in the container. Removes the only environment blocker to running the per-phase gate here.
alternatives: Run e2e only on the host
Skip e2e in the per-phase gate

context: During implementation, found the review system (ADR-018) renders via the same legacy makeSlides->createPageContent->jQuery->#content path epic 001 plans to delete; converting the main path does not free it for removal
choice: Re-scoped epic 001 to the main quiz path only; retain #content/#slide, npm jquery, gsap, mobile-drag-drop, and the legacy renderer for review; deferred their removal to PRD-003. Documented in ADR-023; amended T-070/T-150/T-160/T-180/T-190/T-200/T-210/T-220 and PRD success criteria
rationale: User-approved (AskUserQuestion): keeps epic 001 low-risk and focused on user-visible quality fixes; review conversion later reuses the new components
alternatives: Expand epic 001 to convert review now
Pause for offline review

context: T-060 switches the whole SlideDispatcher to store.setSlide at once, but exercise types are converted to Vue components one phase at a time; un-converted types would have nothing to render them
choice: IndexPage watches the store currentSlide and renders converted types via the <component> switcher, falling back to the legacy slide.makeSlides(document) DOM renderer for un-converted types (reproducing the old begin/next hideExplainIcon and decorate-restore conclude behaviour). Each phase moves a type from fallback to a real component + lockstep e2e update
rationale: Keeps every per-phase yarn test:all gate green during the incremental conversion; the legacy renderer is retained anyway for review (ADR-023)
alternatives: Convert all types before the first gate
Render a placeholder div (breaks the e2e journey)

context: First Pinia store usage (useSlideStore) crashed e2e with getActivePinia() no active Pinia; quasar.config.cjs sourceFiles.store pointed at ./src/vue/store/index (singular, nonexistent) so Quasar never installed Pinia
choice: Fixed sourceFiles.store to ./src/vue/stores/index (the real path). Also added HTMLAudioElement to the eslint globals list
rationale: The store dir is stores/ (plural) and stores/index.ts is a proper Quasar store() wrapper returning createPinia(); fixing the path installs Pinia app-wide for both component and dispatcher store access. Pinia absence was previously harmless because nothing used a store
alternatives: Add a dedicated src/boot/pinia.ts boot file

context: Phase 5 gap/sort use vue-draggable-plus (SortableJS, ADR-020), which listens to pointer events; the Cypress dragDrop helper fires native HTML5 dragstart/drop, which SortableJS ignores
choice: GapExercise implements click-to-place (tap token, tap slot) as the primary/tested interaction (also accessible + mobile-friendly) alongside vue-draggable-plus drag for desktop. The e2e uses a placeToken(token, slot) click helper instead of dragDrop. SortExercise needs no drag in the e2e (the journey just clicks Done).
rationale: Avoids flaky SortableJS+Cypress synthetic-drag simulation while still shipping real drag for users; click-to-place is robust to test and improves accessibility
alternatives: Simulate SortableJS pointer drag in Cypress (flaky)
Native HTML5 DnD instead of SortableJS (diverges from ADR-020)

context: T-230 differential replay: the post-conversion snapshot showed one delta vs the committed baseline — an extra session key highest_reached_test
choice: Verified storage is unchanged by recording a fresh snapshot on main (pre-implementation): it produces the IDENTICAL state including highest_reached_test=27 (modulo the ts timestamp). The committed baseline was stale (predated the review-system highest_reached tracking, which is set by the untouched preAdvanceHook). Refreshed cypress/replay/baselines/example.cy.json to the true current state
rationale: The conversion is storage-neutral: main and the converted branch yield byte-identical localStorage/sessionStorage (save keys, values, cont flags) apart from the timestamp. No silent save-data regression
alternatives: Treat the stale-baseline delta as a regression (false positive)
